channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led effort to halt climate change lawsuits in Democratic-led states
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected a lawsuit from Republican attorneys general in 19 states — including Minnesota —aimed at blocking climate change suits against the oil and gas industry from Democratic-led states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US hasn't determined who was behind attack that caused outage on Trump adviser Musk's social site X
item.description: U.S. officials have not determined who was behind an apparent cyber attack on the social media site X that limited access to the platform for thousands of users.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Katie Porter jumps into California governor's race
item.description: Former Rep. Katie Porter (D-Calif.) announced her bid for California governor on Tuesday, joining a growing field of Democratic contenders in the race to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom (D).
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Dem pushes to censure Boebert over TV interview about Al Green
item.description: Rep. Chrissy Houlahan, D-Pa., submitted a resolution to censure Rep. Lauren Boebert, R-Colo., for "racist and derogatory" remarks about Rep. Al Green, D-Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the White House hired Republican political firms to launch an anti-migrant ad campaign
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security skipped a fully competitive bidding process to give two Republican-linked firms the first part of a $200 million television ad campaign that lauds President Donald Trump for his crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are marching ahead with a government funding bill despite Democratic opposition
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson says lawmakers "have to keep the government in operation” as Republicans face a critical test of their unity.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court seems intent on taking small steps in dealing with challenges to Trump's agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In fewer than 500 carefully chosen and somewhat opaque words, the Supreme Court has now weighed in twice on President Donald Trump's rapid-fire efforts to remake the federal government. The justices did not give Trump's administration what it sought.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem seeks to halt Trump from 'invading' Greenland, Canada and Panama
item.description: The No Invading Allies Act bars funding from going toward armed forces to engage in operations seeking to take over Greenland, Canada and Panama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP frets as Wall Street takes dip over tariffs, talk of recession
item.description: Senate Republicans expressed new worries about the economy Monday after Wall Street had one of its worst days in years amid a trade war and remarks from President Trump that did not rule out the possibility of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas lawmakers considering bill to ban gender changes on birth certificates
item.description: Lawmakers in the Texas Senate are debating a bill that would prohibit people in the state from changing the gender marker on their birth certificate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump, facing terrible coverage, softens tone and tactics for Elon Musk’s DOGE crusade
item.description: Fox News' Howard Kurtz shares why he believes that Donald Trump is acutely attuned to the growing backlash against massive layoffs occurring in the US government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — With a new distillery set to open soon, the makers of Brough Brothers bourbon in Kentucky were ready to put their business plan into action. They were looking to ramp up whiskey production to break into lucrative new markets in Canada and Europe.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump-backed plan to avert shutdown heads for House vote
item.description: House lawmakers are poised to vote on a bill supported by President Donald Trump to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean Curran, agent who protected Trump at Butler rally, sworn in as new US Secret Service director
item.description: President Donald Trump's pick to lead the Secret Service, Sean Curran, was sworn into the role on Monday in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge halts effort to deport Columbia protest leader, sets Wednesday hearing
item.description: A federal judge on Monday scheduled a Wednesday court hearing for Mahmoud Khalil, a recent Columbia University graduate who was arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), forbidding the government from moving forward with deportation efforts before then.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued President Donald Trump's Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing a legally awarded grant.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama and her brother to launch a podcast with weekly guests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Michelle Obama and her brother, Craig Robinson, will host a new weekly podcast series starting this month featuring a special guest pulled from the world of entertainment, sports, health and business.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal cuts likely to hurt economies around nation's capital
item.description: Washington, D.C. is projecting declines in consumer spending and housing demand, as well as $1.01 billion in city revenue lost by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court will take up state bans on conversion therapy for LGBTQ+ children, in a Colorado case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed on Monday in a case from Colorado to decide whether state and local governments can enforce laws banning conversion therapy for LGBTQ+ children.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota House seat gets filled as Legislature begins confronting new budget reality
item.description: Minnesota’s Legislature will see a major change this week with a special election in the House, and will start grappling with the updated budget forecast that showed tighter finances. Both will likely change the dynamic of this legislative session moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation
